author: sourcery-ai
body: **suggestion (bug_risk):** Failure re-printing uses `r.objType`, which may be empty for fetch-error results.\\n\\nIn the early fetch-error path `obj` is never loaded, so `objType` stays at its zero value and the failure summary prints with a blank type. When constructing `verifyResult` on that path, please set `objType` from whatever type info is in `summary`, or use a placeholder like "unknown" so the output remains informative.\\n\\nSuggested implementation:\\n\\n```golang\\n // Print failures after all goroutines complete (avoids interleaved output)\\n for _, r := range results {\\n  if !r.skipped && !r.passed {\\n   objType := r.objType\\n   if objType == "" {\\n    objType = "unknown"\\n   }\\n   fmt.Printf("  ✗  [%-12s] %-30s %s\\n", objType, truncate(r.key, 30), r.message)\\n  }\\n }\\n\\n```\\n\\nTo fully implement your suggestion at the source of the data:\\n1. Locate the “early fetch-error path” where a `verifyResult` is constructed without loading `obj` (likely inside the goroutine that populates `results[i]` before the snippet you provided).\\n2. In that error-handling branch, populate the `objType` field on `verifyResult` from whatever type information is available on `summary` (for example, `summary.Type` or similar). If no such type information exists, explicitly set `objType: "unknown"` there.\\n3. With that change plus the print-time fallback above, fetch errors will always show an informative type and the printing logic remains robust even if some call sites forget to set `objType`.

author: gemini-code-assist
body: ![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)\\n\\nThe `verifyResult` struct is initialized in these error paths without the `objType` field. Since the new reporting loop at line 221 relies on `r.objType` to display the object type for failures, this will result in empty brackets (e.g., `  ✗  []`) in the final output. Consider populating `objType` from the `summary` object (e.g., `summary.Type`) to ensure the failure report remains informative and consistent with the success output.

Ensure all Go code paths that read/write to these tables have been removed or rerouted before applying this migration. Additionally, the Down block should include an explicit statement (e.g., `SELECT 'irreversible migration';`) rather than relying on a comment to make the irreversibility explicit to Goose.\\n\\n<details>\\n<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>\\n\\n```\\nVerify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.\\n\\nIn `@apps/server/migrations/00089_remove_integrations_datasource_githubapp.sql`\\naround lines 1 - 34, Update application code to remove or reroute any live\\nreferences to the dropped tables and mark the migration irreversible in the SQL\\nDown block: remove or refactor usages of the kb.data_source_sync_jobs entity in\\nDataSourceSyncJob (apps/server/domain/extraction/entity.go), update scheduler\\nconfiguration in tasks.go (apps/server/domain/scheduler/tasks.go) to stop\\nscheduling or use the new replacement, and remove health metric references in\\nmetrics_handler.go (apps/server/domain/health/metrics_handler.go); after those\\ncode changes compile and run tests, modify the migration's Down section to\\ninclude an explicit irreversible statement (e.g., a SELECT 'irreversible\\nmigration';) so Goose sees the Down is intentionally non-reversible.\\n```\\n\\n</details>\\n\\n<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:poseidon:nectarine:54b3f12c-c10f-4ed9-b984-cc6811efe965 -->\\n\\n<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

author: coderabbitai
body: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_\\n\\n**Don’t count deleted 404s as verified disk checks.**\\n\\nThis returns before `checkDisk`, so a deleted graph object that 404s is counted as passed even if the corresponding source file/domain still exists on disk. Consider deriving the disk path from summary metadata when available; otherwise mark this as skipped/unverified rather than passed.\\n\\n\\n\\n<details>\\n<summary>🛠️ Proposed safer fallback</summary>\\n\\n```diff\\n    obj, err := g.GetObject(ctx, id)\\n    if err != nil {\\n     // If the object is being deleted and its head ID 404s, it's already gone — treat as pass\\n     if summary.Status == "deleted" && strings.Contains(err.Error(), "404") {\\n-     results[idx] = verifyResult{summary: summary, skipped: false, passed: true, key: summary.CanonicalID, message: "already absent (404)"}\\n+     results[idx] = verifyResult{\\n+      summary: summary,\\n+      skipped: true,\\n+      passed:  false,\\n+      key:     summary.CanonicalID,\\n+      message: "object already absent (404); disk check unavailable",\\n+     }\\n      return\\n     }\\n     results[idx] = verifyResult{summary: summary, skipped: false, passed: false, key: summary.CanonicalID, message: fmt.Sprintf("fetch error (id=%s): %v", id, err)}\\n```\\n\\n</details>\\n\\n<details>\\n<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>\\n\\n```\\nVerify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.\\n\\nIn `@blueprints/code-memory-blueprint/tools/branch-verify/main.go` around lines\\n188 - 195, The current branch in which GetObject returns a 404 for a deleted\\nsummary prematurely marks the verifyResult as passed (in the goroutine handling\\nresults[idx]) without performing the disk check; adjust the logic in the\\nGetObject error branch to avoid returning before running checkDisk: if summary\\nmetadata contains a disk path (use fields on summary/CannonicalID metadata or\\nother storagePath fields), derive the path and run checkDisk(ctx, path) and set\\nverifyResult.passed accordingly; if no disk path can be derived, set\\nverifyResult as skipped/unverified (skipped: true, passed: false) instead of\\npassed:true; update the code around GetObject, summary, checkDisk and\\nverifyResult to reflect these changes.\\n```\\n\\n</details>\\n\\n<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:poseidon:ibis -->\\n\\n<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->
